label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The main task of the IARC, according to the passage. The question is asking which option correctly identifies the main task. First, I need to remember what the IARC does as described in the text. The passage mentions that the IARC classifies carcinogens into five groups. They are part of the WHO and focus on research about cancer. Looking at the options: A says "To focus on cancer-causing substances." The IARC does classify carcinogens, which are substances that cause cancer. So Group 1, 2A, etc., are classifications they do. That seems to align with option A. Option B is about evaluating air quality. The passage does mention evaluating air everyone breathes, but the IARC's main role isn't just about air quality in general. The quote says their task was to evaluate air pollution for cancer risk, but that's part of their broader work on carcinogens. Option C is to study the developing risk of cancer. The IARC's work includes assessing risks, like how air pollution increases lung cancer risk. But their main task isn't just about risk development; their classification system is a key part. Option D is sending signals to the international community. The last quote mentions sending a signal,
